summary: Bryan Cantrill has compiled an extensive archive of his technical talks and recorded conversations spanning nearly two decades, organizing them into thematic trilogies covering debugging, open source, containers, DTrace, and software values. He specifically highlights 'Principles of Technology Leadership' and 'Coming of Age' as his most personally meaningful talks that represent his truest self. The post serves as both a practical index for those wanting to explore his work and a reflection on the themes that have defined his career in systems software.
key_points: Organized talks into thematic trilogies: Software Values, Debugging, Beloved (Papers We Love), Open Source, Containers, and DTrace
Two essential talks recommended above all others: 'Principles of Technology Leadership' and 'Coming of Age'
Career spans from proprietary software at Sun to open source advocacy at Joyent to hardware/software co-design at Oxide
Created Systems We Love conference inspired by Papers We Love, with emphatically double-blind selection process
Oxide and Friends podcast evolved from Twitter Spaces to become a vector for engineering wisdom and community
money_quotes: This potentially self-destructive behavior seems likely to have unwanted side-effects like spontaneous righteous indignation, superfluous historical metaphor, and near-lethal exposure to tangential anecdote – and yet I find myself compelled to enable it by collecting my erstwhile scattered talks.
These are the only talks that I have asked family and friends to watch, as they represent my truest self – or what I aspire that self to be, anyway.
In late 2016 and through 2017, it felt like fundamental values like decency and integrity were under attack; it seems appropriate that these three talks were born during this turbulent time.
While my career started developing proprietary software, I am blessed that most of it has been spent in open source.

In hindsight, the moment that I flipped the double bird was the moment that I forever lost moral authority with my children, who somehow managed to find that clip online and have never let me live it down.
key_insight: A technologist's body of work, when curated and organized thematically, reveals the through-lines of values and passions that define not just a career but an identity.
